title: Authorization via tx.origin — a relaying contract passes the owner check (phishing)

params.failDetail: Authorization uses tx.origin. tx.origin is the ORIGINAL external account, so if the owner is tricked into calling a malicious contract, that contract can relay the call and this check passes. Use msg.sender (the immediate caller) for authorization.
params.passDetail: Authorization does not rely on tx.origin — msg.sender (the immediate caller) is the correct identity to check.

provenance.note: tx.origin auth is a canonical Solidity phishing footgun the docs explicitly warn against; agents still emit it because require(tx.origin == owner) reads as 'only the owner'.
